title: AI Product Roadmap
context: AI startup figuring out what to build next
prompt: We launched our AI writing tool 6 months ago. Users want: better outputs, more templates, team features, API access, and integrations. What do we build next?
response:
Okay, I hear you. Six months in, and your users are giving you clear signals on what they want next: better outputs, more templates, team features, API access, and integrations. That's a good problem to have – clear demand!

Before we dive into prioritizing, could you give me a little more context on a few things?

1.  Who are your primary users right now? (e.g., individual freelancers, small marketing teams, large enterprises, specific niches?)
2.  What's your current business model? (e.g., free, freemium, subscription per user, usage-based?)
3.  What's the single most important goal for your product right now? (e.g., user acquisition, retention, increasing revenue per user, entering a new market?)

This will help us figure out which of these requests will move the needle most for your business.

In the meantime, I'll save these user requests so we don't lose track of them.Got it. I've noted down those user requests: better outputs, more templates, team features, API access, and integrations.

To help figure out what to build next, could you tell me a bit more about:

1.  Who are your main users today? (e.g., individual writers, small marketing agencies, large content teams, specific industries?)
2.  How do you currently charge for your tool? (e.g., free, freemium, monthly subscription per user, usage-based?)
3.  What's the single most important thing you're trying to achieve with your product right now? (e.g., get more users, keep existing users, make more money per user, expand into a new market?)
